name: Poly Moog
explanation: A fat, warm polyphonic analog synthesizer with up to eight independent voices. Each voice runs three detuned oscillators (two band-limited saws and a pulse) into a 4-pole ladder-style low-pass with tanh saturation, swept by its own filter envelope, then an amplitude envelope; chords ring as separate fat voices. Controls: Detune, Cutoff, Resonance, Filter Env Amount, Attack, Release and Level.
